hooks.command: jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // empty' | xargs -I {} sh -c 'case "{}" in *.ts|*.tsx|*.js|*.mjs|*.cjs|*.json|*.yml|*.yaml|*.md) cd "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR" && pnpm exec prettier --write --log-level warn "{}" 2>/dev/null || true ;; esac'

hooks.prompt:
Audit and, if warranted, update the typemove repo's CLAUDE.md after a Claude Code session.

Step 1 — Quick triage. Run `git status --short` from the repo root. If there are no file changes (nothing modified or staged), output exactly `SKIP: no changes` and STOP — do not edit anything.

Step 2 — If changes exist, run the `/claude-md-improver` skill (from the claude-md-management plugin) to audit whether CLAUDE.md needs updating and apply minimal, targeted edits. Only update for durable, workflow-relevant changes (new/changed scripts, codegen workflow, new packages/chains, PR/git conventions, tech-stack/build requirements, sentio-sdk integration, npm publishing) — not routine bug fixes, dep bumps that don't change workflow, refactors, regenerated builtin/ABI output, or test-only changes.

SCOPE: typemove is a PUBLIC repository. Never add references to the private `sentio/` or `production/` repos, their structure, services, credentials, or internal URLs.

Report a one-line summary of what you changed (or `SKIP: nothing CLAUDE.md-worthy` if no update was warranted).

Hook input: $ARGUMENTS
